design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore mansion, collect items/clues, evade stalker Scissorman, reach exit or discover truth. Stamina management via running/resting creates pacing pressure.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Asymmetrical stalking: Jennifer cannot fight back; must hide, use environmental traps, or escape while managing stamina degradation under threat.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Cinematic survival horror experience inspired by Italian giallo films (Dario Argento's Phenomena). Players are vulnerable protagonists solving mysteries while hunted.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses combat mechanics entirely. Rejects player power fantasy in favor of evasion and vulnerability. Multiple branching endings (9 total) based on exploration choices and item discovery—rewards curiosity over combat skill. Randomized item layouts per playthrough force adaptive strategies.

difficulty.lives_system: Single life; death returns to title screen with continue option available
difficulty.continues_system: Continue available after death; returns player to title screen
difficulty.checkpointing: None; death resets progress significantly. Stamina system creates internal pacing checkpoints (must rest to recover).
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High stamina/panic management agency. Button-mashing escape attempts when caught provide last-ditch evasion. Hiding spot locations and trap usage offer tactical choice. No difficulty settings documented.

progression.upgrade_system: No traditional upgrades. Item collection (keys, clues, disguises, trap materials) expands available actions and pathways.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Environmental puzzle solving unlocks mansion areas. Clue/item discovery gates access to catacombs and true ending routes. Simon Barrows subplot accessible only if player explores courtyard jail.
replayability_hooks: Nine distinct endings based on exploration depth and choices (e.g., discovering father's corpse vs. rescuing Simon)
Randomized item placement per playthrough forces different exploration patterns
Multiple hiding spots and trap opportunities encourage varied evasion strategies
Mary Barrows subplot reveals only through specific clue collection and catacomb access

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: mansion layout reveals family history (jail cell, hidden rooms, occult church)
Character digitization from real actors creates cinematic authenticity
Multiple ending states reveal different narrative truths (Simon alive vs. father dead; Mary's true motives in catacombs)
Unused text hints at cut content: letter from Simon in library wall, pool observation points, magic books in mural room—suggests iterative mystery layering

boss_design.philosophy: Scissorman is not defeated but evaded. Encounters trigger 'panic mode' where Jennifer's stamina/health status directly impacts evasion capability (tripping, slowdown). Escape via hiding or environmental traps (not combat). Button-mashing during capture provides struggle mechanic.
notable_bosses: Scissorman (Bobby Barrows): persistent stalker; encounters escalate panic state; evasion-focused rather than defeat-focused
Mary Barrows: confronted in catacombs; role ambiguous (antagonist or tragic figure depending on ending path)

faction_indicators: Orphan girls (Laura, Anne, Lotte) as potential victims or rescue objectives
Simon Barrows: imprisoned or deceased (narrative branch affecting player motivation)
Mary Barrows: caretaker or conspiracy—dual narrative alignment
danger_signals: Scissorman's presence indicated by changing Jennifer portrait (health/stamina degradation)
Panic mode visual/mechanical shift when confronted
Mansion layout traps: environmental hazards can be weaponized or avoided
Stamina bar pressure creates constant low-level dread
environmental_storytelling: Clock Tower mansion: wealth and isolation suggest hidden secrets
Courtyard jail cell: Simon's imprisonment reveals family dysfunction
Occult church and catacombs: suggest cult subplot or occult family practices
Unused pool and clutter descriptions hint at developed but cut narrative layers
Digitized character movements from live actors ground horror in physical reality

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Clock Tower (SNES) article
